---
title: "Customer Churn Prediction Analysis"
subtitle: "CIS 9660 - Group 2 Project Proposal"
date: "March 16, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    logo: ""
    footer: "CIS 9660 · Group 2 Project · Spring 2026"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    highlight-style: github
    code-block-bg: true
    incremental: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.08
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#1F3864"
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Team Members {background-color="#1F3864"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Our Team
- **Harrison Cabe**
- **Raúl J. Solá Navarro**
- **Samuel Spitzer**
- **Victor Murra Schott**

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Course
CIS 9660 - Data Mining

**Proposal Due:** March 20, 2026  
**Presentation:** March 16, 2026
:::
:::

---

## Project Overview {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## The Big Question
**Can we predict whether a telecom customer will churn based on their contract type, monthly charges, and service usage?**
:::

<br>

| Dimension | Focus |
|-----------|-------|
| 📊 **Domain** | E-commerce & Retail - Telecom Customer Retention |
| 🎯 **Task Type** | Binary Classification (Churn: Yes / No) |
| 🧪 **Primary Method** | Logistic Regression with Interaction Terms |

---

## Dataset {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Kaggle: Telco Customer Churn

🔗 [`kaggle.com/datasets/blastchar/telco-customer-churn`](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/blastchar/telco-customer-churn)

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Observations | **7,043 customers** |
| Variables | **21 features** |
| Target | `Churn` (Yes / No) |
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Feature Groups
**Demographics**  
Gender, Senior Citizen, Partner, Dependents

**Services**  
Phone, Internet, Streaming, Security

**Account**  
Contract, Tenure, Monthly/Total Charges, Payment Method
:::
:::

---

## Key Variables {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 🎯 Target
`Churn` - Binary: **Yes / No**

### 📌 Primary Predictors
- `Contract` - Month-to-month, One year, Two year  
- `MonthlyCharges` - Continuous (USD)  
- `InternetService` - DSL, Fiber Optic, None  
- `tenure` - Months with company  
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### 📎 Additional Features
- `TotalCharges`
- `PaymentMethod`
- `PaperlessBilling`
- `OnlineSecurity`, `TechSupport`
- `StreamingTV`, `StreamingMovies`
:::
:::

---

## Research Question {background-color="#1F3864"}

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}
*Can we predict customer churn from*  
*contract type and monthly charges?*
:::

<br>

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 🔮 Predictive
Build a model that accurately classifies customers as likely to **churn** or **stay**
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 🔬 Inferential
Does the **interaction** between `InternetService` × `tenure` significantly affect churn probability?
:::
:::

---

## Methods: EDA {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

### Step 1 - Exploratory Data Analysis

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
**📦 Boxplots**  
Compare `MonthlyCharges` and `tenure` distributions across churn classes
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**🔵 Scatterplots**  
Explore relationships between continuous variables (`MonthlyCharges`, `tenure`, `TotalCharges`) and churn
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**📊 Bar Charts**  
Churn rates segmented by `ContractType`, `InternetService`, and `PaymentMethod`
:::
:::

<br>

> **Tools:** `pandas`, `matplotlib`, `seaborn`

---

## Methods: Logistic Regression {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

### Step 2 - Binary Logistic Regression

::: incremental
1. **Baseline model** - `ContractType` + `MonthlyCharges` + `InternetService` + `tenure`

2. **Interaction term** - `ContractType × InternetService` to test synergy effects on churn probability

3. **Model evaluation** - Confusion matrix, Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-Score, ROC-AUC

4. **Cross-validation** - k-fold CV to assess generalizability
:::

<br>

> **Tools:** `scikit-learn`, `statsmodels`

---

## Expected Findings {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 📈 Hypotheses
- **Month-to-month** contract customers will have significantly higher churn rates
- **Higher MonthlyCharges** correlates with increased churn risk
- **Fiber optic + short tenure** customers may show elevated churn (interaction effect)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 📐 Model Performance Targets
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Accuracy | ≥ 80% |
| ROC-AUC | ≥ 0.80 |
| Recall (Churn=Yes) | ≥ 75% |

*Recall prioritized — cost of missing a churner is high*
:::
:::

---

## Practical Implications {background-color="#1F3864"}

<br>

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 🎯 Targeted Retention
Send personalized offers - e.g., upgrade a month-to-month user to a **discounted annual contract**

### 💰 Pricing Strategy
Restructure pricing tiers or offer **loyalty discounts** to high-charge, high-risk customers
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 📈 CLV Optimization
Retaining customers longer directly **increases Customer Lifetime Value**

### 🔧 Service Improvement
High churn among fiber optic users may signal **service quality issues** to investigate
:::
:::

---

## Project Timeline {background-color="#f7f9fc"}

| Milestone | Date | Deliverable |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| 🗣️ In-Class Presentation | March 16, 2026 | Slides (email by March 15) |
| 📋 Project Proposal | March 20, 2026 | 1–2 page writeup (Brightspace) |
| 💻 Final Presentation | May 11, 2026 | 10-min talk + Q&A |
| 📄 Final Report | May 15, 2026 | 4–5 page report + Python code + data |
| ⭐ Peer Evaluation | May 15, 2026 | 0–5 scale per teammate |

---

## Summary {background-color="#1F3864"}

<br>

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
**Dataset:** Telco Customer Churn (Kaggle)  
`7,043 obs × 21 variables`

**Question:** Can contract type & monthly charges predict churn?

**Method:** Logistic Regression + Interaction Terms  
`ContractType × InternetService`

**Goal:** Help marketing teams proactively retain at-risk customers
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}

<br>

:::
:::
